author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I was able to take a closer look at this and there is a bug in the builder where the parent block hash of the bid and the envelope can be different if the payload for the previous slot arrives really late.
In this case, the payload for slot 65423 was received by the buildoor node a few ms into slot 65424. So the builder actually built a payload for the parent block hash of slot 65422 as the payload for 65423 was received which is `0x8befbe2b1578f81e4efd293e080e8b820052225542d604b48ca28b7d557c76dc` but set the parent block hash of the bid to `0x829a91c3784b7ef8b4831c12b82a808031282764a60d9c210febe7f92fcd4454` basically picking up the parent block hash from the head block's(slot 65423) bid block hash. Which explains the difference in the parent hashes. 
Even tho the payload for slot 65423 arrived ms into the slot 65424, it still somehow received >50% of PTC votes. I am not sure why my buildoor beacon node(prysm node) received the payload envelope so late? Were there some networking latency issues in the devnet?
The payload for slot 65424 was correctly reorged by lodestar in slot 65426: https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-3.ethpandaops.io/slot/65426
